title: Where things live in the menu
intro: A map of every screen, grouped by whether it's always available (Core) or needs a sub-module switched on.

blocks.title: Core — visible with permission, on any branch
Sub-modules — need the branch module ON, plus permission

blocks.text: Imports have no dedicated menu item. COA and opening balance uploads happen in the general Import/Export area; only approving the staged batch happens on an accounting route.

title: Setting up a new branch
intro: Follow this order for a clean go-live — each step depends on the one before it.

blocks.title: Recommended setup sequence
items: Enable the sub-modules this branch actually needs — this unlocks the relevant menu items and routes.
Configure Financial Settings — currency, retained earnings account, cutover date, tax defaults. Everything downstream depends on this.
Create or import the Chart of Accounts — accounts must exist before anything can point to them.
Review Account Mappings — either the seeded defaults or your own custom setup.
Review Posting Rules — this is what actually turns events into journals.
Create a Fiscal Year that covers your go-live date — posting needs an open year to land in.
Import opening balances, then validate and approve — this loads your starting Trial Balance without double-counting anything already happening operationally.
Configure Tax Types, if you're using the tax module.
Optional extras: bank accounts, cost centres, petty cash, and so on.
Run one test sale or GRN in a sandbox and confirm it shows up as Completed with the right numbers.

blocks.title: If you skip a step, here's what breaks
rows: Posting fails: “fiscal period closed” or no fiscal year covers the date
Posting fails: “could not resolve account for mapping key …”
The event is marked Skipped — no journal, no error
Journals dated before cutover may be blocked
No starting balances appear — only new operational postings

title: Chart of Accounts
intro: The backbone of the whole system. Every single journal line points to exactly one account here.

blocks.title: What each field means
rows: A unique account ID, used in imports and reports.
What shows on journals and reports.
Asset / Liability / Equity / Revenue / Expense — decides which statement it lands on.
Optional — should point to a group (header) account.
Yes = header only, can't receive postings.
Yes = journal lines can actually use it.
Optional — scopes the account to one branch.
Inactive accounts are hidden from pickers but keep their history.

items.cause: An account code doesn't exist
items.effect: Any import, mapping, or posting that references it fails with a validation error.

items.cause: You try to post to a group account
items.effect: Blocked — group accounts are headers only, never postable.

items.cause: A duplicate code exists in the same branch
items.effect: Import validation fails; manual creation may also error.

items.cause: An account has the wrong type
items.effect: Reports get misclassified. Fix it with a reversal and remapping — never by editing posted lines.

items.cause: There's no Chart of Accounts at all
items.effect: No journals can be created, and auto-posting fails the moment it tries to resolve an account.

title: Account Mappings
intro: Mappings are the translation layer — they connect a logical idea, like “sales revenue,” to a real account number, and can get more specific by branch, warehouse, or payment method.

blocks.title: How the system picks which mapping to use
items: It loads every active mapping for that key.
It filters down by branch, warehouse, payment method, or currency — whatever's present in the event.
It respects each mapping's effective date range.
It picks the most specific match — a branch-plus-warehouse mapping beats a global one.

items.cause: No mapping exists for a key
items.effect: Auto-posting fails, and you'll see the error on the Accounting Events screen.

items.cause: A mapping points to an inactive account
items.effect: It's skipped during resolution — and may fail entirely if there's no fallback.

items.cause: Two mappings are equally specific
items.effect: The one with the lower priority number wins.

items.cause: The wrong mapping was used
items.effect: Journals post to the wrong account. Reverse the journal and fix the mapping before reprocessing.

title: Posting Rules
intro: A posting rule is the recipe: one event type in, a set of debit and credit lines out.

blocks.title: Rule set fields
rows: Which business event triggers this rule
The date range this rule applies
When several rules could match, the higher priority wins

blocks.title: What each line inside a rule defines
rows: Payment method account, mapping key, tax account, bank account, fixed account…
If false, the line is simply skipped when its amount is zero

blocks.text: Net effect: cash goes up, revenue and tax get credited, cost of goods sold gets debited, and inventory gets credited down — all in one balanced journal, automatically.

items.cause: No rule exists for an event type
items.effect: The event is marked Skipped — this is expected behavior, not an error.

items.cause: A required line can't find an account
items.effect: The event fails, with a resolution error explaining why.

items.cause: A line's amount is zero and it's marked optional
items.effect: It's simply left out — the journal can still balance fine.

items.cause: Several active rules could match
items.effect: The highest-priority match for that date and branch wins.

items.cause: You edit a rule
items.effect: Only future events use the new version — already-posted journals never change.

title: Financial Settings
intro: The global defaults everything else builds on — currency, control accounts, journal behavior, and tax defaults, all on one page.

blocks.title: General
rows: Your reporting currency for every GL amount
Used to suggest new fiscal years
Earliest date live posting is allowed
How long an approved reopen stays open — 48 hours by default

blocks.title: GL control accounts
rows: Required before you can close a year
A temporary holding spot when a rule can't resolve an account cleanly
Absorbs penny differences on multi-line journals

blocks.text: Retained earnings not set? Fiscal year close will abort with a clear error the moment you try.

blocks.title: Journal & tax controls
rows: If off, only system-generated journals can post at all
Amounts above this need approval before posting
Used when a sale doesn't specify one
Used when a purchase event doesn't specify one
Monthly / Quarterly / Annual — for reporting cadence

blocks.text: Fiscal years are managed on this same page — list, close, request-reopen, and approve/reject reopen requests all live here.

title: Fiscal years
intro: Fiscal years control when posting is allowed at all — every journal date has to fall inside an open one.

blocks.title: Statuses
rows: A brief transitional state during year-end
Every journal in that year is locked
A temporary window after dual approval

blocks.title: What happens when you close a year
items: The system checks the year is open, has no unposted drafts, no unbalanced posted journals, and that retained earnings is configured.
Status flips to Closing.
Every journal in the year gets locked.
Net income — revenue minus expenses — is calculated from posted, non-closing entries.
If it's material, a closing journal transfers that net income into retained earnings.
Status flips to Closed, and who closed it plus when is recorded.

blocks.text: If close fails, the year stays open (or reverts from Closing) and the error tells you exactly why — most often an unposted draft sitting in that year.

blocks.title: Reopening a closed year
items: Someone submits a reopen request with a reason.
A first approver — a different user — signs off.
A second approver signs off — status flips to Reopening and journals unlock.
A countdown starts, based on your configured reopen window.
When it expires, the year automatically locks itself back to Closed.

items.cause: A reopen request is rejected
items.effect: It's marked rejected, and the year stays closed.

items.cause: You try to post after the reopen window expires
items.effect: Blocked, with an error naming the fiscal year and its status.

blocks.title: How other features respect the fiscal year
rows: journal_date must fall inside an open year (or a valid reopen window)
Same rule — uses the event or sale date
Cutover date and fiscal year must both allow the import date
The reversal date itself must land in an open period

title: Journal entries (manual)
intro: For anything that isn't auto-posted — corrections, one-off adjustments, or entries with no operational trigger.

blocks.title: Creating one
items: Journal Entries → New.
Enter the date, description, and branch.
Add lines — account, and either a debit or a credit, not both, per line.
Save as Draft.
Submit for approval if the amount is over your configured limit.
Post once it's approved and balanced.

blocks.text: Before posting, the system always checks: at least one line exists, debits equal credits to two decimal places, the fiscal period is open, and every account used is postable and active.

blocks.title: Reversing a posted journal
items: Open the posted journal → Reverse.
The system creates a new draft with the opposite debits and credits, dated whenever you choose.
That reversal posts through the same checks as any other journal.
The original is marked Reversed.

blocks.text: The reversal's fiscal year is worked out from the reversal date you pick — not copied from the original entry's year.

items.cause: A draft doesn't balance
items.effect: Posting is blocked, and the error shows both totals and the exact difference.

items.cause: You try to post into a closed fiscal year
items.effect: Blocked — with a detailed error naming the year and its status.

items.cause: You try to edit a posted journal
items.effect: Not allowed at all — reverse it instead.

items.cause: Manual posting is switched off in Settings
items.effect: Manual entries can't post, even if they're balanced and approved.

title: Accounting events (automatic posting)
intro: Every attempt to auto-post something leaves a trace here — a complete audit trail of operational activity turning into (or failing to turn into) the General Ledger.

blocks.title: Event statuses
rows: If stuck past ~5 minutes, the system auto-recovers it for retry
Nothing — click through to the linked journal if you like
Something went wrong building or posting it
Read the error, fix the config, then Retry
No active posting rule matched this event type
Add a rule, or ignore it if that's intentional

blocks.title: The pipeline, step by step
items: An operational module fires an event — a sale completes, for example.
A listener hands it to the Accounting Event pipeline.
An idempotency check runs first — if this exact event already completed, the existing journal is returned and nothing new is created.
If no posting rule matches, the event is marked Skipped.
Otherwise the Posting Rule Engine builds the journal lines.
A draft journal is created and posted in a single database transaction.
The event is marked Completed, linked to the journal it produced.

blocks.text: Retry is available on any Failed event, once you've fixed whatever caused it — a missing mapping, a closed fiscal year, whatever the error pointed to.

items.cause: The same sale fires twice (double-click, retried webhook)
items.effect: The second attempt finds the already-Completed event and returns it — no second journal is ever created.

items.cause: A mapping is missing
items.effect: Failed, with a “could not resolve account…” error.

items.cause: The fiscal year for that date is closed
items.effect: Failed, with a period-lock message.

items.cause: Tax amount is positive but no tax type is set
items.effect: The default from Financial Settings may be used — or the tax line may fail if the tax accounts themselves are missing.

items.cause: Inventory cost comes out to zero
items.effect: Optional COGS lines are simply skipped if they're marked not required.

title: Tax configuration & posting
menu: Accounting → Tax Types (needs the tax module)
intro: How a tax rate turns into a properly separated liability or asset line on your journals.

blocks.title: Tax type fields
rows: Exclusive (added on top) or Inclusive (baked in)
The liability account for tax you've collected
The asset account for tax you've paid
On purchases, how much input tax can actually be claimed back

blocks.title: How tax actually reaches the GL
items: An operational event includes a tax amount and, optionally, a tax type in its payload.
A posting rule line with amount source Tax amount and resolution Tax account stamps that tax type onto the journal line.
For purchases, only the recoverable portion of input tax posts to the input tax asset account.

items.cause: The tax module is disabled for this branch
items.effect: The Tax Types menu is hidden — but tax lines may still post fine if the rules and mappings already exist.

items.cause: No tax type is configured at all
items.effect: Tax lines may fail, or post without a tax type, depending on how the rule is written.

items.cause: Tax was disabled at POS checkout
items.effect: The tax amount comes through as zero, so any optional tax lines get skipped automatically.

items.cause: The output tax account is wrong
items.effect: Your tax liability is misstated — fix the tax type, then reverse and reprocess if needed.

title: Cost centres
menu: Accounting → Cost Centres (needs the cost_centres module)
intro: An optional tag you can put on a journal line to see profit and loss by department, project, or store — not a replacement for the account itself, just an extra dimension.

blocks.title: Setting one up
items: Create your cost centre hierarchy — code, name, optional parent, optional branch.
Configure your posting rules (or manual journals) to actually tag lines with a cost_centre_id where it's relevant.

blocks.text: The Cost Centre P&L report, under Financial Reports, rolls up revenue and expense by cost centre.

items.cause: The module is disabled
items.effect: The menu is hidden, but lines still post normally — just without a cost centre attached.

items.cause: A line has no cost centre
items.effect: It only shows up in the consolidated P&L, not any per-centre breakdown.

title: Imports — COA & opening balances
intro: Both import types follow the same shape: upload, validate, then explicitly approve. Nothing hits your live books just from uploading a file.

blocks.title: Chart of Accounts import
rows: Each row validated — code, type, parent, branch
Valid rows are written into the Chart of Accounts

blocks.text: Typical CSV columns: code, name, type, parent_code, is_group, is_postable, branch_code, currency_code, status. A row that fails validation is flagged with a message — the batch can stay Failed until you fix and re-import it.

blocks.title: Opening balance import
rows: Each line checked: postable account, debit XOR credit, header balances
For AR/AP batches, party-level totals are compared against the control account
A single balanced opening journal is created, flagged as an opening balance, and posted

blocks.text: Variance tolerance: if your AR or AP sub-ledger total doesn't match the control account within about a cent, the batch stays unreconciled until an authorized user explicitly approves the variance.

items.cause: Debits and credits don't balance
items.effect: The batch fails outright — no journal is created.

items.cause: Someone without permission tries to approve
items.effect: 403 Forbidden.

items.cause: The cutover date is after the journal date
items.effect: Posting is blocked at approval time.

items.cause: An account in the file doesn't exist in the COA
items.effect: That line fails validation.

items.cause: You approve the same COA batch twice
items.effect: Safe — it upserts by code, so nothing duplicates.

title: Sub-modules
intro: Each of these is opt-in per branch. Here's what each one actually does and where to find it.

blocks.title: 16.1 Credit notes (needs credit_notes + ar_ap)
rows: Issue a credit note against a customer, which reduces what they owe
credit_note.issued → credits AR, debits revenue or a dedicated account per your rule

blocks.text: If credit_notes is enabled but ar_ap isn't, the Credit Notes module stays off — both are required together.

blocks.title: 16.2 Debit notes — no standalone Accounting page
rows: Procurement → Purchase Returns → issue a debit note on an approved return
Reduces AP / adjusts the purchase accrual, per the seeded posting rule
Available directly from the purchase return / debit note route

blocks.text: Looking for “Debit Notes” in Accounting and can't find it? That's by design — it lives under Procurement, not Accounting. If posting fails, check Accounting Events for a Failed debit_note.issued row and troubleshoot it the same way as any other event.

blocks.title: 16.3 Bank accounts & reconciliation (needs bank_reconciliation)
rows: Link a real bank account to a COA account
Import a statement, then match unmatched lines to journal transactions or create adjustments

blocks.text: A bank account that isn't linked to a COA account can't be reconciled — that link is required.

blocks.title: 16.4 Multi-currency (needs multi_currency)
rows: Maintain currency codes and exchange rates
Journals can hold both the transaction currency and the converted functional-currency amount
A service can post unrealized gain/loss journals at period end

blocks.text: If an exchange rate is missing for a currency, conversion simply fails for any event in that currency.

blocks.title: 16.5 Petty cash (needs petty_cash)
rows: One register per branch or imprest holder, tracking top-ups, disbursements, and adjustments
petty_cash.* events, if you've wired up rules for them

blocks.title: 16.6 Cheques (needs cheques)
rows: Each status change can fire an accounting event, if a rule is configured for it

blocks.title: 16.7 Fixed assets (needs fixed_assets)
rows: Asset categories define depreciation accounts; assets get capitalized

blocks.text: If the depreciation accounts aren't configured, the depreciation post simply fails.

title: Financial reports
intro: Everything here is built from what's actually posted — drafts never appear until they're finalized.

blocks.title: What's available
rows: Every account's debits and credits — must always net to zero
Revenue and expenses over a period
Assets, liabilities, and equity as of a date
Line-by-line detail per account, with a running balance
Drafts not yet reflected in the Trial Balance
Every journal in the selected period

blocks.text: Common filters: date range, fiscal year, branch, account or account range, and cost centre where relevant. CSV export needs the accounting.export-reports permission.
Draft journals never appear in the Trial Balance or P&L until they're posted.

title: How operational modules connect
intro: A module-by-module map of exactly which action triggers which accounting event, and what it typically does to your books.

blocks.title: 18.1 Sales / POS
rows: Dr Cash/Card · Cr Revenue · Cr Output tax · Dr COGS · Cr Inventory
Reverses the revenue/AR/tax/inventory components per your rule

blocks.text: If tax is switched off at checkout, tax rule lines simply skip (the amount is zero). Payment-method settlement lines use the payment_method_account mapping for whichever method was used.

blocks.title: 18.2 Procurement
rows: Dr Inventory · Cr GRNI/clearing or AP
Dr Expense/Inventory · Dr Input tax · Cr AP
Reduces AP / adjusts the supplier balance — issued from Purchase Returns, see §16.2

blocks.title: 18.3 Inventory
rows: Dr/Cr Inventory vs. an adjustment expense
Inter-warehouse inventory move, if rules exist

blocks.text: Where COGS comes from: on a sale, the inventory cost comes straight from your cost layers — FIFO or WAC, resolved by the Cost Service.

blocks.title: 18.4 Customers / AR
rows: Dr Bad debt expense · Cr AR

blocks.text: AR Aging is an operational, sub-ledger report — the GL's AR control account should reconcile against it once opening balances and all AR events are in.

title: End-to-end examples
intro: Four common real-world walkthroughs, start to finish.

blocks.title: 19.1 Bringing a new tenant live
items: Enable the modules this branch needs on its branch profile.
Set Financial Settings — currency USD, retained earnings account 3200, cutover date 2026-07-01.
Import the Chart of Accounts CSV and approve the batch.
Import opening balances as of 2026-06-30, validate, and approve.
Create FY2026 (Jan–Dec) as an open year.
Post a test sale on 2026-07-02 and confirm the event shows Completed with the right numbers on the Trial Balance.

blocks.title: 19.2 A normal retail day
items: Every cashier sale fires its own sale.completed event, all day.
Next morning, the accountant filters Accounting Events for anything Failed.
Weekly, someone pulls the Trial Balance for the week.
Monthly: P&L review, bank reconciliation, and the AP payment run.

blocks.title: 19.3 Closing the year
items: Post every remaining adjusting journal.
Run the Unposted Journals report — it needs to come back empty.
Financial Settings → Close fiscal year.
Verify the closing journal actually landed in retained earnings.
The new fiscal year is already open, ready for next period's postings.

blocks.title: 19.4 Fixing a mis-posted sale
items: Find the journal — either through Journal Entries or the link from the original sale / event.
Reverse it, dated today.
If the cause is systemic, fix the underlying mapping or rule too.
Add a manual adjusting journal only if this was a genuine one-off.
Never edit the original posted journal directly.

blocks.text: Example 19.5 — a failed event after go-live: open the failed row in Accounting Events, read the error (e.g. “could not resolve account for mapping key output_tax”), add the missing mapping, click Retry, and confirm it comes back Completed and balanced.

title: Permissions reference
intro: For support staff diagnosing “I can't see / can't do X” tickets.

blocks.title: Full permission list
rows: View Chart of Accounts, journals, and events
Create and edit the Chart of Accounts
Financial Settings and fiscal year CRUD

blocks.text: The seeded Accountant role typically holds most of these. Cashier doesn't see the Accounting section at all.

title: Troubleshooting & FAQ
intro: The fastest way to diagnose the most common support tickets.

blocks.title: Navigation problems
rows: tax module off for this branch
Enable it on the branch accounting profile
The whole Accounting section is gone

blocks.title: Posting & events
rows: No posting rule for that event type
Create or activate a rule set
Fix the amounts or sides in the posting rule
Fiscal year closed, or before cutover
Reopen the year, or adjust the date
Missing mapping, or an inactive account
Fix the mapping, or reactivate the account
Rare — would indicate an idempotency bug
Check whether two events actually had different source IDs
Wait ~5 minutes, or retry — stale recovery resets it automatically

blocks.title: Journals
rows: The approver needs to approve it first
Can't post to a certain date
Request a reopen, or use the current year

blocks.title: Imports
rows: Invalid type, parent, or duplicate code
Fix the flagged rows shown in the batch
Approve the variance, or fix the party-level lines
Normal — approval is a separate step
Run the approve action on the batch

blocks.title: Reports
rows: Trial Balance doesn't net to zero
A data issue, or unposted drafts included
Run it on posted entries only, then check for corrupted lines
No postings in that range, or wrong branch
Widen the date range, check the branch filter
General Ledger is empty for an account
No posted lines in that range
Post journals, or expand the date range

blocks.title: Fiscal year
rows: Draft journals still sitting in that year
Set the account in Financial Settings
Year isn't closed yet, or a request is already pending
Submit a new reopen request, or post in the current year instead

blocks.text: Enabling sub-modules today (interim): there's no on-screen toggle yet — an admin sets BranchAccountingProfile.accounting_enabled_modules directly (e.g. via php artisan tinker), then the user selects that branch and refreshes.

blocks.title: Frequently asked questions
terms: Does a sale fail if accounting posting fails?
No — the sale still completes. Check Accounting Events for a Failed status and retry once the config issue is fixed.
Can I delete a posted journal?
In Journal Entries — filter by the opening balance flag, or the description left by the import batch.
Why is COGS showing as zero on a sale?
Either there are no cost layers yet (no prior GRN/receive history), or the optional COGS line was skipped because cost came out to zero.
No — that's a separate, later module.
Where are debit notes in Accounting?
There isn't a Debit Notes menu — they're issued from Procurement → Purchase Returns. The GL effect still shows up via debit_note.issued in Accounting Events and Journal Entries.
Can a customer pay on account without a new sale?
A standalone “payment received” event is still on the roadmap — today it's partially covered through the sale's own settlement lines.
